value: In pi TUI sessions, prefix bash commands with ! instead of exiting pi. E.g., !ls, !pwd, !kill. No need to exit/restart pi for shell commands.
Tmux gotcha: sending C-c multiple times rapidly triggers tmux copy mode (pink bar "jump forward"). Fix: send Escape or q to exit copy mode. Always add sleep between C-c sends.

RULE: When running autonomous loop (autopilot mode), NEVER stop. Keep picking tasks from the backlog and executing them until the backlog is empty. Do NOT ask the user if you should continue. Just keep going. When the backlog is empty, find new work to do (scan for TODOs, improve docs, find bugs, etc).

SYNAPTIC MEMORY — A Pi extension that gives the agent persistent, intelligent, evolving memory across all sessions.

The #1 gap in AI coding tools: every session starts from zero. This extension closes that gap.

Core concept:
- WATCHES every tool call, decision, outcome across sessions
- EXTRACTS knowledge: patterns, preferences, project conventions, failed approaches, successful strategies
- STORES in a local semantic graph (not just key-value)
- RECALLS relevant context on session start, injected into system prompt
- LEARNS what's useful by tracking which memories get referenced
- DECAYS old/unused memories automatically

This makes the agent genuinely smarter over time. It's not just a scratch pad — it's an evolving knowledge layer.

Key differentiators from scratch-pad:
1. Automatic extraction (no manual saving)
2. Semantic association (related memories linked)
3. Relevance scoring (what mattered in similar contexts before)
4. Decay/growth (useful memories strengthen, unused ones fade)
5. Cross-project learning (patterns from one project help another)

Tools: recall (LLM searches memory), remember (LLM explicitly stores), forget (LLM removes)
Commands: /memory (browse), /memory-stats (see graph health), /memory-export (backup)
